body:
## Verdict

agent_supports: yes
irrlicht_observes: yes

## Reasoning

`full-lifecycle-toolcall` maps to coverage_id `auto-executed-tool-call` (scenario-meanings.md): the agent invokes one or more tools mid-turn, every call auto-resolves with no user approval, and the turn completes â€” the user-observable arc is `ready â†’ working â†’ ready` and state NEVER enters `waiting`. Its primitive is `tool_calls`; it requires `headless_mode` and `tool_calls`.

**agent_supports: yes.** Codex CLI 0.130.0 invokes shell/tool calls autonomously and runs them without an approval gate â€” `replaydata/agents/codex/capabilities.json` records `tool_calls: true` and `permission_hooks: false`, so a prompt that asks codex to run a shell command produces a real tool round-trip that auto-resolves without ever blocking on the user. The coverage matrix already records codex/auto-executed-tool-call as yes/yes.

**irrlicht_observes: yes.** The codex parser (parser.go) translates `function_call` and `function_call_output` transcript events into `tailer` tool-use / tool-result records, and `task_complete` event_msg into `turn_done`, so the daemon keeps the session `working` across the tool call and settles it `working â†’ ready` at turn end via the primary `IsAgentDone()` path. Because codex has no permission hooks, no `waiting` transition is emitted â€” matching the scenario's negative invariant. The sibling interactive cells multi-turn-conversation (10/10) and agent-question-pending (7/7) confirm the daemon observes codex's live-REPL workingâ†”ready arcs cleanly.

## Recipe-shape note (interactive, not headless)

The scenarios.json codex stub for this cell is a thin headless `prompt`. Headless `codex exec` is single-shot: the turn-end surfaces as `process_exited` on the rollout session rather than a `working â†’ ready` flip, and a short turn is debounced so the `ready â†’ working` start is coalesced â€” basic-turn (headless) lands only 3/5, with its `turn_start` working phase failing. This cell is therefore recorded with the INTERACTIVE driver (`send` a tool-using prompt â†’ `wait_turn` â†’ trailing `sleep`); the live REPL keeps the rollout session alive across the turn so the daemon observes the full workingâ†’ready arc, then `process_exited` only at teardown. This is the same shape that lets multi-turn-conversation pass 10/10.
caveats: claudecode's variant of this scenario ends in waiting via a PostToolUse permission hook; codex has no such hook (permission_hooks:false), so the codex arc is readyâ†’workingâ†’ready and the spec asserts a no-waiting invariant rather than the claudecode-shaped top-level verify.has_waiting_state.

sources.note: function_call / function_call_output â†’ tool-use/tool-result; task_complete â†’ turn_done so workingâ†’ready settles via primary path
tool_calls: true, permission_hooks: false â€” tools auto-resolve, no waiting gate
auto-executed-tool-call canonical flow: readyâ†’workingâ†’ready, never enters waiting
interactive codex live-REPL turn arcs validated 10/10 â€” the recording shape used here
